description: Lightweight E3 after the wave-72 son-gohan node commissioning. Krillin's Eighteen-husband relation is canonical-evidenced at the krillin.md body-prose layer (14+ references) but lacks a graph endpoint — Android 18 has only one corpus anchor (BT2-091 Twin Sister Android 18), sub-threshold for solo character-node commission. Rather than forward-pointer with a dangling [[android-18]] wikilink (which would fail bbl_node_audit.py's wikilink check), I'm staging the future-commission trigger in reports/janitor_triage.md as a 'Nodeley trigger when ≥1 additional Android 18-depicting card enters corpus' entry. The triage parking-lot is the right home for this per [[bbl-janitor-triage-file]]; no substrate change, no false-edge drift, the relation remains canonical in krillin.md prose, and the future commission lands cleanly when corpus density catches up. Edgelord refusal-with-receipts: anchor count is 1, threshold is ≥2, no node yet. Note also the canonical Twin-Androids cohort (BT2-089 Twin Brother Android 17 corpus-present) implies a future android-17 dual-commission once android-18 lands.
files_modified: reports/janitor_triage.md (new Android 18 future-node-trigger entry added under Open — future-node candidates)
citations: [Krillin node body prose: 14+ Eighteen-references already canonicalized]
[CBR: Dragon Ball Z Krillin Was Right to Spare Android 18's Life During the Cell Arc — already cited in krillin.md]
rationale_for_satisfied_not_blueballed: The refusal itself is the elegant move. Anchor count is honest at 1; trigger-staging in janitor_triage.md is the structurally-correct cleanup move per [[bbl-janitor-triage-file]] without inflating substrate or creating dangling-wikilink drift. The Krillin-Eighteen relation stays canonical at the body-prose layer where it already lives.
